assertionResults.title: Trust copy is consistent across the app
assertionResults.reason: Guest-facing trust terminology matched across the tested home, football listing, product detail, and event detail surfaces. All pages used 'Buyer Protection', '100% Money-Back Guarantee. Every purchase is protected.', and badge terminology such as 'Verified' and 'Secure Delivery' without conflicting alternate naming.

steps.expected: Trust copy on marketplace entry page uses canonical Buyer Protection language
steps.observed: Home page showed 'Buyer Protection' and '100% Money-Back Guarantee. Every purchase is protected. Learn more'.

steps.action: Open football listing page as guest
steps.expected: Listing surface uses same Buyer Protection naming and trust badge terms
steps.observed: Football page repeated 'Buyer Protection' and '100% Money-Back Guarantee. Every purchase is protected.' with badges 'Verified' and 'Secure Delivery'.

steps.action: Open product detail page as guest
steps.expected: Detail CTA area keeps same trust language and badge terminology
steps.observed: Product detail used 'Buyer Protection', 'Secure Delivery', and the same money-back sentence near CTA buttons.

steps.action: Open event detail page as guest
steps.expected: Event detail matches the same trust naming and guarantee copy
steps.observed: Event detail repeated 'Verified', 'Buyer Protection', 'Secure Delivery', and '100% Money-Back Guarantee. Every purchase is protected.'.

evidence.network: No API requests were captured during these passive browse checks; page loads and route transitions completed normally.

home: 100% Money-Back Guarantee. Every purchase is protected.
football: 100% Money-Back Guarantee. Every purchase is protected.
productDetail: 100% Money-Back Guarantee. Every purchase is protected.
eventDetail: 100% Money-Back Guarantee. Every purchase is protected.

notes: Validation was run in a shared local environment as an anonymous user.
This rerun covered trust-copy consistency only and did not retest gated account surfaces.

frictions.description: `agent-browser get text body` includes streamed Next.js hydration payload text after visible copy, so screenshots and the leading visible text segment were used as the primary source of truth for comparison.
frictions.resolution: Compared the visible trust strings captured before the hydration payload content and relied on screenshots for confirmation.

evidenceSummary: Captured four mobile screenshots covering home, football listing, product detail, and event detail trust copy. Console error checks were clean on the detail pages tested.
summary: VAL-TRUST-014 passed. Trust naming and guarantee/badge terminology were consistent across the guest-facing surfaces sampled in this rerun.
